=== PAGE: https://iccns-conference.org/2026/venue.php ===
Link: VENUE
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Conference Venue
THE
CONFERENCE VENUE
The Conference will take place between the 1
st
and the 4
th
September 2025 in the notorious Hotel & Casino "Cherno more",
which is located on bul. "Slivnitsa" 33, PO9000 in Varna, Bulgaria.
Hotel & Casino "Cherno more" takes advantage of its
unique location - both in the heart of the city and only meters away from the
beach and the sea. The hotel is superbly located on the main pedestrian
promenade with many sightseeing and entertainment possibility nearby, including
landmarks and museums, galleries, shops, pubs, clubs and the famous Varna Sea
Garden.
One of the tallest and
most emblematic buildings of Varna, Hotel and Casino “Cherno More”, which
translates to "Black Sea" from Bulgarian language, has celebrated its
45 years in 2023.
The building was
designed by arch. Simeon Dimitrov and was opened on the 24
th
May
1978. The hotel was a large-scale and ambitious project for its time. Located
on the pedestrian promenade in the ideal Centre of Varna, the hotel has always
been closely connected with the cultural life of the city, in its role as a
main partner and place of accommodation for major events - festivals, music
events, conferences and seminars. Hotel & Casino "Cherno More" is
the preferred choice for casual travellers, business trips and events and for
all who wish to visit Varna, the Northern Black Sea and Bulgaria.
The 14-storey Cherno more hotel offers 199 rooms refurbished
elegantly, to a refined, high standard, for a comfortable and enjoyable stay.
All hotel rooms have balconies with wonderful sea and city views. Some of the
hotel rooms can accommodate guests on extra beds. Whether you visit Varna for a
business trip or a vacation near the sea, each of the rooms provides comfort
and coziness suitable for work or leisure. Guests have high-speed internet
access throughout the complex.
THE
CITY OF VARNA
Varna is the third-largest city in Bulgaria and the largest city and
seaside resort on the Bulgarian Black Sea Coast and in the Northern Bulgaria
region. Situated strategically in the Gulf of Varna, the city has been a major
economic, social and cultural centre for almost three millennia. Historically
known as Odessos, Varna developed from a Thracian seaside settlement into a
major seaport on the Black Sea.
Varna is an important centre for business, transportation,
education, tourism, entertainment, and healthcare. The city is referred to as
the maritime capital of Bulgaria and has the headquarters of the Bulgarian Navy
and merchant marine. In 2008, Varna was designated as the seat of the Black Sea
Euroregion by the Council of Europe. In 2014, Varna was awarded the title of
European Youth Capital.
The city landmarks include the Varna Archaeological Museum,
exhibiting the golden treasures of Varna, the Roman Baths, the Battle of Varna
Park Museum, the Naval Museum in the Italianate Villa Assareto, which is
displaying the museum ship and former torpedo boat “Drazki”, the Museum of
Ethnography in an Ottoman-period compound, which is featuring the life of local
urban dwellers, fisherfolk, and peasants in the late 19
th
and early
20
th
century. The 'Sea Garden' of Varna is the oldest and perhaps
largest park in the town that contains an open-air theatre, the Varna Aquarium,
which was opened in 1932, the Festa Dolphinarium, which was opened in 1984, the
Nicolaus Copernicus Observatory and Planetarium, the Museum of Natural History,
a terrarium, a zoo, an alpineum, a children's amusement park with a pond,
boathouse and ice-skating rink, as well as other attractions.
ENTERING
BULGARIA
Prior
to your journey, please pay attention that since 1 January 2007, the Republic
of Bulgaria is part of the European Union (EU) and is implementing the Common
Visa Policy of the EU.
According
to the EU Council Decision 2024/210 of 30 December 2023 on the full application
of the provisions of the Schengen acquis in the Republic of Bulgaria and
Romania, as of 31 March 2024, the Republic of Bulgaria is part of the Schengen
area by initially abolishing checks on persons at internal air and sea borders.
According to EU Council Decision 2024/210 of 12 December 2024 setting the date
for the lifting of checks on persons at internal land borders with and between
the Republic of Bulgaria and Romania, as of 1 January 2025, the checks on
persons at internal land borders have been lifted.
As
the Republic of Bulgaria is part of the EU and the Schengen Area, some guests
and the participants from specific countries might need to get visa for
entering the country. More information about the visa for Bulgaria can be found
at the webpage of the Bulgarian Ministry of Foreign Affairs:
https://www.mfa.bg/en/services-travel/consular-services/travel-bulgaria/visa-bulgaria
INVITATION
LETTERS
Invitation
letters will be issued to all participants following the acceptance of their
papers! For the issuing of the Invitation Letter you must provide the title of
the paper and a valid copy/photo of your International passport. Please send
all inquiries and questions to email:
pzahariev@uni-ruse.bg

VARNA INTERNATIONAL
AIRPORT, VARNA, BULGARIA
Varna International
Airport is approximately 11 km away from Hotel & Casino “Cherno More”. Bus
line №409 will take you directly from the bus terminal at the airport to the

https://www.varnatraffic.com/en/Line/Routes/409
SOFIA INTERNATIONAL

Sofia International
Airport is located in the Capitol of Bulgaria and is approximately 500 km away

nd

Link: CAMERA READY
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Camera ready Guidelines
The Deadline for submitting the
Camera-ready
version for the Main Track Papers
is: August 12, 2025 (Extended)
The below instructions are essential to ensure that you comply with the publication requirements set by IEEE in order for your paper(s) to be included in the 
						conference proceedings and published and indexed by IEEE.
Please make sure to incorporate all the reviewers' comments as that will be strictly monitored. In order to ensure that your paper is 
						included in the conference proceedings and will be submitted to appear in the IEEE Xplore, at least one of the authors must register and present the paper 
						either onsite or online.
The Final Camera Ready System
ICCNS is using the
PayQlick System
for both Camera ready submission and Registration.
Camera Ready Submission
1. Manuscript Format and Length
Your PDF final manuscript version and the electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF) must be submitted through
PayQlick System
. Please make sure your paper length is
8 pages for full papers, 6 pages for short papers and 2 pages for poster papers (all including references)
. Papers that exceed the length limit must pay the overlength charges.
Please make use of the appropriate IEEE template for conference proceedings to prepare your revised manuscript. Failur to do so may result in excluding your paper from the conference proceedings.
The needed IEEE Word template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Word Template)
.
The needed IEEE Latex template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Latex Template)
.
DO NOT include headers/footers or page numbers in the final submission.
2. Registartion
Please complete your paper(s) registration on-time. Registration instructions and rates are found at:
https://events.payqlick.com/event/41/ieee-iccns-2025
NO PAPER WILL BE PUBLISHED WITHOUT THE AUTHOR’S COMPLETED REGISTRATION AND PAYMENT.
3. Generate an IEEE XPLORE Compatible Manuscript Using PDF XPRESS
All final manuscripts must be generated using the PDF Xpress tool. Upload your camera-ready manuscript in one of many formats supported by the service and download your validated PDF when ready. To generate the IEEE Xplore compatible PDF file of your paper, follow these steps:
Log in to the IEEE PDF eXpress site:
https://ieee-pdf-express.org/
First-time users should do the following:
Select create account
Enter the following:
66249X
for the Conference ID
Your email address
A password
Continue to enter information as prompted. An Online confirmation will be displayed and an email confirmation will be sent verifying your account setup.
Once you have registered as a new user:
Upload the source file (containing your paper) for conversion.
Receive by e-mail the IEEE Xplore-compatible PDF of your paper for download.
If your paper coversion is declined (Not approved by IEEE), the system will provide with the errors log. You need to fix it, and re-do the same process.
Previous users of PDF eXpress need to follow the above steps, but should enter the same password that was used for previous conferences. Verify that your contact information is valid.
4. Electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF)
The authors should complete the Electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF) which is available through
PayQlick System
. Once you complete your registration:
Go to view My Tickets
Submit your IEEE eCF
5. Camera-Ready version Submission
Finally, you should upload your IEEE Xplore compatible PDF manuscript to
PayQlick System
.
Go to view My Tickets
Upload your Camera Ready (Must be IEEE PDF Certified)
If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to contact us at:
info@iccns-conference.org

Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Call for Papers
SUBMIT A PAPER
With the advancements in wireless communication systems like Fifth Generation (5G), beyond 5G (B5G) and the Sixth Generation (6G), 
 new and unprecedent services will be available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver of the future digital transformation of our cities and 
 communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquities deployment of the Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of 
 the network and at the cloud. The computing infrastructure will be handling the processing of the data generated by the users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require
 an efficient and sustainable applications running on the computing\Networking infrastructure and also a smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components. 
 Artificial Intelligence (AI)  and its applications will play a significant role on the design, deployment, automation and management of the future services.  This will include the applications
 that will be running on the edge and the cloud servers, the networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system and the intelligent automation and 
 management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present the state of the 
 art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking and Services that is supported by Artificial Intelligence.

Paper format

.

.

.

.
Length
A full paper must be of maximum length of 8 pages (including all figures,

Originality
Papers submitted to ICCNS must be the original work of the authors. 
					The may not be simultaneously under review elsewhere. Publications that have been peer-reviewed 
					and have appeared at other conferences or workshops may not be submitted to ICCNS. Authors should 
					be aware that IEEE has a strict policy with regard to plagiarism
https://www.ieee.org/publications/rights/plagiarism/plagiarism-faq.html
The authors' prior work must be cited appropriately.

					allowed to be changed in any way after the paper submission deadline.

					the language use  in the paper is clear and correct so that it is easily understandable.

Link: CALLS
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Call for Papers
SUBMIT A PAPER
With the advancements in wireless communication systems like Fifth Generation (5G), beyond 5G (B5G) and the Sixth Generation (6G), 
 new and unprecedent services will be available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver of the future digital transformation of our cities and 
 communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquities deployment of the Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of 
 the network and at the cloud. The computing infrastructure will be handling the processing of the data generated by the users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require
 an efficient and sustainable applications running on the computing\Networking infrastructure and also a smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components. 
 Artificial Intelligence (AI)  and its applications will play a significant role on the design, deployment, automation and management of the future services.  This will include the applications
 that will be running on the edge and the cloud servers, the networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system and the intelligent automation and 
 management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present the state of the 
 art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking and Services that is supported by Artificial Intelligence.
Researchers from both the industry and academia are encouraged to submit their original research contributions in all major areas, which include, but not limited to:

Call for Workshop Proposals
ICCNS will host a series of workshops and is also inviting new workshop proposals. 
				The purpose of the workshops is to emphasize current and emerging topics of particular interest to the community.
Proposal Format
Each workshop proposal must include (maximum 4 pages, in PDF format):
Title of the workshop.
Workshop organizers (name, affiliation and short biography).
Scope and topics of the workshop.
Rationale:
Why the workshop is related to ICCNS.
Why the topic is important.
Why the workshop may attract a significant number of attendees.
Workshop details:
A draft call for papers (including organizers, program committee, and steering committee if any). Organizers are expected to be fully committed and physically present at the workshop.
Workshop tentative schedule (number of expected papers, duration full/half day, format talks/posters, etc.). We encourage full-day events that demonstrate the interest of the community in the proposed topic and guarantee the commitment of the organizers. Half-day proposals are also welcome but expected to provide a rationale for the proposed duration.
Names of potential participants and invited speakers (if any).
Workshop history: if there are past workshops, the history of the workshop.
Proposals and any other enquiries should be sent to
info@iccns-conference.org

Title: ICCNS2024
ICCNS2024
The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2024)
24-27 Sept. 2024 | DUBROVNIK, CROATIA.
Call for Papers
With the advancements in wireless communication systems like Fifth Generation (5G), beyond 5G (B5G) and the Sixth Generation (6G), new and unprecedent services will be available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver of the future digital transformation of our cities and communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquities deployment of the Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of the network and at the cloud. The computing infrastructure will be handling the processing of the data generated by the users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require an efficient and sustainable applications running on the computing\Networking infrastructure and also a smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components.  Artificial Intelligence (AI)  and its applications will play a significant role on the design, deployment, automation and management of the future services.  This will include the applications that will be running on the edge and the cloud servers, the networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system and the intelligent automation and management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present the state of the art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking and Services that is supported by Artificial Intelligence.

Welcome to The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Comunications, Networking and Services (ICCNS2023)
Call for Papers
New advancements in wireless communication systems such as Fifth-Generation (5G), Beyond Fifth-Generation (B5G), and Sixth-Generation (6G) networks will allow for new and unprecedented services to be made available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver for future digital transformation of our cities and communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquitous deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of the network and in the cloud. This computing infrastructure will handle the processing of data generated by users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require the applications running on the computing/networking infrastructure to be Intelligent, efficient and sustainable. Additionally, the infrastructure will require smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and its applications will play a significant role in the design, deployment, automation, and management of future services. This will include applications that will be running on the edge and on cloud servers, networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system, and intelligent automation and management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking, and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present state of the art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking, and Services that are supported by Artificial Intelligence.

=== PAGE: https://iccns-conference.org/2026/venue.php ===
Link: VENUE
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Conference Venue
THE
CONFERENCE VENUE
The Conference will take place between the 1
st
and the 4
th
September 2025 in the notorious Hotel & Casino "Cherno more",
which is located on bul. "Slivnitsa" 33, PO9000 in Varna, Bulgaria.
Hotel & Casino "Cherno more" takes advantage of its
unique location - both in the heart of the city and only meters away from the
beach and the sea. The hotel is superbly located on the main pedestrian
promenade with many sightseeing and entertainment possibility nearby, including
landmarks and museums, galleries, shops, pubs, clubs and the famous Varna Sea
Garden.
One of the tallest and
most emblematic buildings of Varna, Hotel and Casino “Cherno More”, which
translates to "Black Sea" from Bulgarian language, has celebrated its
45 years in 2023.
The building was
designed by arch. Simeon Dimitrov and was opened on the 24
th
May
1978. The hotel was a large-scale and ambitious project for its time. Located
on the pedestrian promenade in the ideal Centre of Varna, the hotel has always
been closely connected with the cultural life of the city, in its role as a
main partner and place of accommodation for major events - festivals, music
events, conferences and seminars. Hotel & Casino "Cherno More" is
the preferred choice for casual travellers, business trips and events and for
all who wish to visit Varna, the Northern Black Sea and Bulgaria.
The 14-storey Cherno more hotel offers 199 rooms refurbished
elegantly, to a refined, high standard, for a comfortable and enjoyable stay.
All hotel rooms have balconies with wonderful sea and city views. Some of the
hotel rooms can accommodate guests on extra beds. Whether you visit Varna for a
business trip or a vacation near the sea, each of the rooms provides comfort
and coziness suitable for work or leisure. Guests have high-speed internet
access throughout the complex.
THE
CITY OF VARNA
Varna is the third-largest city in Bulgaria and the largest city and
seaside resort on the Bulgarian Black Sea Coast and in the Northern Bulgaria
region. Situated strategically in the Gulf of Varna, the city has been a major
economic, social and cultural centre for almost three millennia. Historically
known as Odessos, Varna developed from a Thracian seaside settlement into a
major seaport on the Black Sea.
Varna is an important centre for business, transportation,
education, tourism, entertainment, and healthcare. The city is referred to as
the maritime capital of Bulgaria and has the headquarters of the Bulgarian Navy
and merchant marine. In 2008, Varna was designated as the seat of the Black Sea
Euroregion by the Council of Europe. In 2014, Varna was awarded the title of
European Youth Capital.
The city landmarks include the Varna Archaeological Museum,
exhibiting the golden treasures of Varna, the Roman Baths, the Battle of Varna
Park Museum, the Naval Museum in the Italianate Villa Assareto, which is
displaying the museum ship and former torpedo boat “Drazki”, the Museum of
Ethnography in an Ottoman-period compound, which is featuring the life of local
urban dwellers, fisherfolk, and peasants in the late 19
th
and early
20
th
century. The 'Sea Garden' of Varna is the oldest and perhaps
largest park in the town that contains an open-air theatre, the Varna Aquarium,
which was opened in 1932, the Festa Dolphinarium, which was opened in 1984, the
Nicolaus Copernicus Observatory and Planetarium, the Museum of Natural History,
a terrarium, a zoo, an alpineum, a children's amusement park with a pond,
boathouse and ice-skating rink, as well as other attractions.
ENTERING
BULGARIA
Prior
to your journey, please pay attention that since 1 January 2007, the Republic
of Bulgaria is part of the European Union (EU) and is implementing the Common
Visa Policy of the EU.
According
to the EU Council Decision 2024/210 of 30 December 2023 on the full application
of the provisions of the Schengen acquis in the Republic of Bulgaria and
Romania, as of 31 March 2024, the Republic of Bulgaria is part of the Schengen
area by initially abolishing checks on persons at internal air and sea borders.
According to EU Council Decision 2024/210 of 12 December 2024 setting the date
for the lifting of checks on persons at internal land borders with and between
the Republic of Bulgaria and Romania, as of 1 January 2025, the checks on
persons at internal land borders have been lifted.
As
the Republic of Bulgaria is part of the EU and the Schengen Area, some guests
and the participants from specific countries might need to get visa for
entering the country. More information about the visa for Bulgaria can be found
at the webpage of the Bulgarian Ministry of Foreign Affairs:
https://www.mfa.bg/en/services-travel/consular-services/travel-bulgaria/visa-bulgaria
INVITATION
LETTERS
Invitation
letters will be issued to all participants following the acceptance of their
papers! For the issuing of the Invitation Letter you must provide the title of
the paper and a valid copy/photo of your International passport. Please send
all inquiries and questions to email:
pzahariev@uni-ruse.bg
TRAVEL
INFORMATION
The Conference will
take place between the 1
st
and the 4
th
September 2025 in
the notorious Hotel & Casino "Cherno more", which is located on
bul. "Slivnitsa" 33, PO9000 in Varna, Bulgaria -
https://maps.app.goo.gl/33BoFjkjfHySKdr97
, GPS Coordinates: 43°20’50.0″N  and 27°91’99.7″E - (DD)
43.20500, 27.91997. You can reach the venue, from:
VARNA INTERNATIONAL
AIRPORT, VARNA, BULGARIA
Varna International
Airport is approximately 11 km away from Hotel & Casino “Cherno More”. Bus
line №409 will take you directly from the bus terminal at the airport to the
closest possible bus station near the hotel – Station “Sevastopol”, which is
just 350 meters away from the venue. More info about the buses you can find
here:
https://www.varnatraffic.com/en/Line/Routes/409
SOFIA INTERNATIONAL
AIRPORT, SOFIA, BULGARIA
Sofia International
Airport is located in the Capitol of Bulgaria and is approximately 500 km away

minutes and 6 hours) or by using public transport. More information here:

BURGAS AIRPORT, BURGAS,
BULGARIA
Burgas Airport is located
in the second largest city on the Bulgarian seacoast and is approximately 120
km away from the city of Varna. You can reach Varna from the Burgas Airport
either by renting a car (approximate travel time between 2 hours and 2 hours
and 30 minutes) or by using public transport. More information here:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/dZEGMcPQTXnJUSWG8
HENRI COANDA
INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT, BUCHAREST, ROMANIA
Bucharest International
Airport is located approx. 300 km away from Varna. The airport, named Henri
Coanda International Airport, is located in Otopeni, 16.5 km north of the Bucharest
city centre. The airport supports flights from more than 30 companies and is
the best solution to reach Varna by airplane. You can reach Varna from the Bucharest
International Airport either by renting a car (approximate travel time between
4 hours and 5 hours) or by using public transport. More information here:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/XYSkVvKp9Pti9SxU9
ISTANBUL HAVALIMANI
AIRPORT, ISTANBUL, TURKEY
Istanbul Havalimani
Airport is the larger of two international airports serving Istanbul, Turkey.
It is located in the Arnavutköy district on the European side of the city. It
is the largest airport in Turkey and the 2
nd
busiest airport in
Europe as well as the 2
nd
busiest airport in the Middle East.
You can reach Varna
from the Istanbul Havalimani Airport either by renting a car (approximate
travel time between 6 hours and 8 hours) or by using public transport. More
information here:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/GCmdpdHRmM8NAqBXA
If you are
having travel-related questions or if you need us to assist you with your
travel – please contact a member of the local organization committee:

pzahariev@uni-ruse.bg
, mobile: +359 82 888 663 and tel.: +359 896 698 699
ACCOMMODATION

MORE”

of the Hotel -

or here:
https://www.booking.com/hotel/bg/hotel-casino-cherno-more.en-gb.html
ROSSLYN
DIMYAT HOTEL VARNA
The Rosslyn Dimyat
Hotel Varna is located in the central part of our seaside capital, conveniently
located between some of the most important key sites and attractions in the
city, making it an even more attractive place to stay during your business trip
or seaside holiday.
The hotel is just a
step away from Varna's Sea Garden, famous for its green areas, beautiful flower
alleys and tennis courts. Most of the hotel rooms offer a magnificent sea view
which you can enjoy up close during your walk along the coast just minutes walking
distance from the hotel.
Rooms can be
booked at the official webpage of the Hotel -
https://dimyat.rosslyn-hotels.com/en/

Link: CAMERA READY
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Camera ready Guidelines
The Deadline for submitting the
Camera-ready
version for the Main Track Papers
is: August 12, 2025 (Extended)
The below instructions are essential to ensure that you comply with the publication requirements set by IEEE in order for your paper(s) to be included in the 
						conference proceedings and published and indexed by IEEE.
Please make sure to incorporate all the reviewers' comments as that will be strictly monitored. In order to ensure that your paper is 
						included in the conference proceedings and will be submitted to appear in the IEEE Xplore, at least one of the authors must register and present the paper 
						either onsite or online.
The Final Camera Ready System
ICCNS is using the
PayQlick System
for both Camera ready submission and Registration.
Camera Ready Submission
1. Manuscript Format and Length
Your PDF final manuscript version and the electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF) must be submitted through
PayQlick System
. Please make sure your paper length is
8 pages for full papers, 6 pages for short papers and 2 pages for poster papers (all including references)
. Papers that exceed the length limit must pay the overlength charges.
Please make use of the appropriate IEEE template for conference proceedings to prepare your revised manuscript. Failur to do so may result in excluding your paper from the conference proceedings.
The needed IEEE Word template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Word Template)
.
The needed IEEE Latex template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Latex Template)
.
DO NOT include headers/footers or page numbers in the final submission.
2. Registartion
Please complete your paper(s) registration on-time. Registration instructions and rates are found at:
https://events.payqlick.com/event/41/ieee-iccns-2025
NO PAPER WILL BE PUBLISHED WITHOUT THE AUTHOR’S COMPLETED REGISTRATION AND PAYMENT.
3. Generate an IEEE XPLORE Compatible Manuscript Using PDF XPRESS
All final manuscripts must be generated using the PDF Xpress tool. Upload your camera-ready manuscript in one of many formats supported by the service and download your validated PDF when ready. To generate the IEEE Xplore compatible PDF file of your paper, follow these steps:
Log in to the IEEE PDF eXpress site:
https://ieee-pdf-express.org/
First-time users should do the following:
Select create account
Enter the following:
66249X
for the Conference ID
Your email address
A password
Continue to enter information as prompted. An Online confirmation will be displayed and an email confirmation will be sent verifying your account setup.
Once you have registered as a new user:
Upload the source file (containing your paper) for conversion.
Receive by e-mail the IEEE Xplore-compatible PDF of your paper for download.
If your paper coversion is declined (Not approved by IEEE), the system will provide with the errors log. You need to fix it, and re-do the same process.
Previous users of PDF eXpress need to follow the above steps, but should enter the same password that was used for previous conferences. Verify that your contact information is valid.
4. Electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF)
The authors should complete the Electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF) which is available through
PayQlick System
. Once you complete your registration:
Go to view My Tickets
Submit your IEEE eCF
5. Camera-Ready version Submission
Finally, you should upload your IEEE Xplore compatible PDF manuscript to
PayQlick System
.
Go to view My Tickets
Upload your Camera Ready (Must be IEEE PDF Certified)
If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to contact us at:
info@iccns-conference.org

Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Call for Papers
SUBMIT A PAPER
With the advancements in wireless communication systems like Fifth Generation (5G), beyond 5G (B5G) and the Sixth Generation (6G), 
 new and unprecedent services will be available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver of the future digital transformation of our cities and 
 communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquities deployment of the Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of 
 the network and at the cloud. The computing infrastructure will be handling the processing of the data generated by the users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require
 an efficient and sustainable applications running on the computing\Networking infrastructure and also a smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components. 
 Artificial Intelligence (AI)  and its applications will play a significant role on the design, deployment, automation and management of the future services.  This will include the applications
 that will be running on the edge and the cloud servers, the networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system and the intelligent automation and 
 management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present the state of the 
 art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking and Services that is supported by Artificial Intelligence.
Researchers from both the industry and academia are encouraged to submit their original research contributions in all major areas, which include, but not limited to:

Submission Guidelines:
SUBMIT A PAPER
Paper format
All papers should be in PDF format. Please make use of the appropriate IEEE template for conference proceedings to 
				prepare your revised manuscript.
Failur to do so may result in excluding your paper from the conference proceedings
.
The needed IEEE Word template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Word Template)
.
The needed IEEE Latex template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Latex Template)
.
DO NOT include headers/footers or page numbers in the final submission
.
Length
A full paper must be of maximum length of 8 pages (including all figures, 
				tables and references). The reference pages must only contain references. Up to 2 aditional pages can be added for extra cost. Overlength papers will be rejected without review.
Originality
Papers submitted to ICCNS must be the original work of the authors. 
					The may not be simultaneously under review elsewhere. Publications that have been peer-reviewed 
					and have appeared at other conferences or workshops may not be submitted to ICCNS. Authors should 
					be aware that IEEE has a strict policy with regard to plagiarism
https://www.ieee.org/publications/rights/plagiarism/plagiarism-faq.html
The authors' prior work must be cited appropriately.
Author list
Please ensure that you submit your papers with the full and final 
					list of authors in the correct order. The author list registered for each submission is not 
					allowed to be changed in any way after the paper submission deadline.
Proofreading:
Please proofread your submission carefully. It is essential that 
					the language use  in the paper is clear and correct so that it is easily understandable. 
					(Either US English or UK English spelling conventions are acceptable.)
IMPORTANT: No-Show Policy
At least one author of each accepted paper is required to register 
					to the conference and present the paper. Only registered and presented papers will be published in 
					the conference proceedings. A paper not presented or presented by a non-author without prior written 
					approval by the TPC Chair will be removed from the final conference proceedings and no registration 
					refund can be made to the authors of these papers.
Publication:
Accepted papers will be submitted for inclusion into IEEE Xplore subject to meeting IEEE Xplore’s scope and quality requirements.
Earlier versions of the proceedings can be found on:
IEEEXplore

Title of the workshop.
Workshop organizers (name, affiliation and short biography).
Scope and topics of the workshop.
Rationale:
Why the workshop is related to ICCNS.
Why the topic is important.
Why the workshop may attract a significant number of attendees.
Workshop details:
A draft call for papers (including organizers, program committee, and steering committee if any). Organizers are expected to be fully committed and physically present at the workshop.
Workshop tentative schedule (number of expected papers, duration full/half day, format talks/posters, etc.). We encourage full-day events that demonstrate the interest of the community in the proposed topic and guarantee the commitment of the organizers. Half-day proposals are also welcome but expected to provide a rationale for the proposed duration.
Names of potential participants and invited speakers (if any).
Workshop history: if there are past workshops, the history of the workshop.

https://www.ieee.org/publications/rights/plagiarism/plagiarism-faq.html
The authors' prior work must be cited appropriately.

Proofreading:

					the language use  in the paper is clear and correct so that it is easily understandable.

IMPORTANT: No-Show Policy
At least one author of each accepted paper is required to register 
					to the conference and present the paper. Only registered and presented papers will be published in 
					the conference proceedings. A paper not presented or presented by a non-author without prior written 
					approval by the TPC Chair will be removed from the final conference proceedings and no registration 
					refund can be made to the authors of these papers.

Call for Workshop Proposals
ICCNS will host a series of workshops and is also inviting new workshop proposals.

Proposal Format
Each workshop proposal must include (maximum 4 pages, in PDF format):
Title of the workshop.
Workshop organizers (name, affiliation and short biography).
Scope and topics of the workshop.
Rationale:
Why the workshop is related to ICCNS.
Why the topic is important.
Why the workshop may attract a significant number of attendees.
Workshop details:
A draft call for papers (including organizers, program committee, and steering committee if any). Organizers are expected to be fully committed and physically present at the workshop.
Workshop tentative schedule (number of expected papers, duration full/half day, format talks/posters, etc.). We encourage full-day events that demonstrate the interest of the community in the proposed topic and guarantee the commitment of the organizers. Half-day proposals are also welcome but expected to provide a rationale for the proposed duration.
Names of potential participants and invited speakers (if any).
Workshop history: if there are past workshops, the history of the workshop.
Proposals and any other enquiries should be sent to
info@iccns-conference.org

Title: ICCNS2023
ICCNS2023
The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2023)
June 19-22, 2023 - Valencia, Spain

Welcome to The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Comunications, Networking and Services (ICCNS2023)
Call for Papers
New advancements in wireless communication systems such as Fifth-Generation (5G), Beyond Fifth-Generation (B5G), and Sixth-Generation (6G) networks will allow for new and unprecedented services to be made available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver for future digital transformation of our cities and communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquitous deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of the network and in the cloud. This computing infrastructure will handle the processing of data generated by users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require the applications running on the computing/networking infrastructure to be Intelligent, efficient and sustainable. Additionally, the infrastructure will require smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and its applications will play a significant role in the design, deployment, automation, and management of future services. This will include applications that will be running on the edge and on cloud servers, networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system, and intelligent automation and management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking, and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present state of the art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking, and Services that are supported by Artificial Intelligence.

=== PAGE: https://iccns-conference.org/2026/venue.php ===
Link: VENUE
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Conference Venue
THE
CONFERENCE VENUE
The Conference will take place between the 1
st
and the 4
th
September 2025 in the notorious Hotel & Casino "Cherno more",
which is located on bul. "Slivnitsa" 33, PO9000 in Varna, Bulgaria.
Hotel & Casino "Cherno more" takes advantage of its
unique location - both in the heart of the city and only meters away from the
beach and the sea. The hotel is superbly located on the main pedestrian
promenade with many sightseeing and entertainment possibility nearby, including
landmarks and museums, galleries, shops, pubs, clubs and the famous Varna Sea
Garden.
One of the tallest and
most emblematic buildings of Varna, Hotel and Casino “Cherno More”, which
translates to "Black Sea" from Bulgarian language, has celebrated its
45 years in 2023.
The building was
designed by arch. Simeon Dimitrov and was opened on the 24
th
May
1978. The hotel was a large-scale and ambitious project for its time. Located
on the pedestrian promenade in the ideal Centre of Varna, the hotel has always
been closely connected with the cultural life of the city, in its role as a
main partner and place of accommodation for major events - festivals, music
events, conferences and seminars. Hotel & Casino "Cherno More" is
the preferred choice for casual travellers, business trips and events and for
all who wish to visit Varna, the Northern Black Sea and Bulgaria.
The 14-storey Cherno more hotel offers 199 rooms refurbished
elegantly, to a refined, high standard, for a comfortable and enjoyable stay.
All hotel rooms have balconies with wonderful sea and city views. Some of the
hotel rooms can accommodate guests on extra beds. Whether you visit Varna for a
business trip or a vacation near the sea, each of the rooms provides comfort
and coziness suitable for work or leisure. Guests have high-speed internet
access throughout the complex.
THE
CITY OF VARNA
Varna is the third-largest city in Bulgaria and the largest city and
seaside resort on the Bulgarian Black Sea Coast and in the Northern Bulgaria
region. Situated strategically in the Gulf of Varna, the city has been a major
economic, social and cultural centre for almost three millennia. Historically
known as Odessos, Varna developed from a Thracian seaside settlement into a
major seaport on the Black Sea.
Varna is an important centre for business, transportation,
education, tourism, entertainment, and healthcare. The city is referred to as
the maritime capital of Bulgaria and has the headquarters of the Bulgarian Navy
and merchant marine. In 2008, Varna was designated as the seat of the Black Sea
Euroregion by the Council of Europe. In 2014, Varna was awarded the title of
European Youth Capital.
The city landmarks include the Varna Archaeological Museum,
exhibiting the golden treasures of Varna, the Roman Baths, the Battle of Varna
Park Museum, the Naval Museum in the Italianate Villa Assareto, which is
displaying the museum ship and former torpedo boat “Drazki”, the Museum of
Ethnography in an Ottoman-period compound, which is featuring the life of local
urban dwellers, fisherfolk, and peasants in the late 19
th
and early
20
th
century. The 'Sea Garden' of Varna is the oldest and perhaps
largest park in the town that contains an open-air theatre, the Varna Aquarium,
which was opened in 1932, the Festa Dolphinarium, which was opened in 1984, the
Nicolaus Copernicus Observatory and Planetarium, the Museum of Natural History,
a terrarium, a zoo, an alpineum, a children's amusement park with a pond,
boathouse and ice-skating rink, as well as other attractions.
ENTERING
BULGARIA
Prior
to your journey, please pay attention that since 1 January 2007, the Republic
of Bulgaria is part of the European Union (EU) and is implementing the Common
Visa Policy of the EU.
According
to the EU Council Decision 2024/210 of 30 December 2023 on the full application
of the provisions of the Schengen acquis in the Republic of Bulgaria and
Romania, as of 31 March 2024, the Republic of Bulgaria is part of the Schengen
area by initially abolishing checks on persons at internal air and sea borders.
According to EU Council Decision 2024/210 of 12 December 2024 setting the date
for the lifting of checks on persons at internal land borders with and between
the Republic of Bulgaria and Romania, as of 1 January 2025, the checks on
persons at internal land borders have been lifted.
As
the Republic of Bulgaria is part of the EU and the Schengen Area, some guests
and the participants from specific countries might need to get visa for
entering the country. More information about the visa for Bulgaria can be found
at the webpage of the Bulgarian Ministry of Foreign Affairs:
https://www.mfa.bg/en/services-travel/consular-services/travel-bulgaria/visa-bulgaria
INVITATION
LETTERS
Invitation
letters will be issued to all participants following the acceptance of their
papers! For the issuing of the Invitation Letter you must provide the title of
the paper and a valid copy/photo of your International passport. Please send
all inquiries and questions to email:
pzahariev@uni-ruse.bg
TRAVEL
INFORMATION
The Conference will
take place between the 1
st
and the 4
th
September 2025 in
the notorious Hotel & Casino "Cherno more", which is located on

43.20500, 27.91997. You can reach the venue, from:
VARNA INTERNATIONAL
AIRPORT, VARNA, BULGARIA
Varna International
Airport is approximately 11 km away from Hotel & Casino “Cherno More”. Bus
line №409 will take you directly from the bus terminal at the airport to the
closest possible bus station near the hotel – Station “Sevastopol”, which is
just 350 meters away from the venue. More info about the buses you can find
here:
https://www.varnatraffic.com/en/Line/Routes/409
SOFIA INTERNATIONAL
AIRPORT, SOFIA, BULGARIA
Sofia International
Airport is located in the Capitol of Bulgaria and is approximately 500 km away
from the city of Varna. You can reach Varna from the Sofia International
Airport either by renting a car (approximate travel time between 5 hours and 30
minutes and 6 hours) or by using public transport. More information here:

BURGAS AIRPORT, BURGAS,
BULGARIA
Burgas Airport is located

HENRI COANDA
INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT, BUCHAREST, ROMANIA
Bucharest International
Airport is located approx. 300 km away from Varna. The airport, named Henri

city centre. The airport supports flights from more than 30 companies and is
the best solution to reach Varna by airplane. You can reach Varna from the Bucharest
International Airport either by renting a car (approximate travel time between

ISTANBUL HAVALIMANI
AIRPORT, ISTANBUL, TURKEY
Istanbul Havalimani
Airport is the larger of two international airports serving Istanbul, Turkey.
It is located in the Arnavutköy district on the European side of the city. It
is the largest airport in Turkey and the 2
nd
busiest airport in
Europe as well as the 2
nd
busiest airport in the Middle East.
You can reach Varna
from the Istanbul Havalimani Airport either by renting a car (approximate

Link: CAMERA READY
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Camera ready Guidelines
The Deadline for submitting the
Camera-ready
version for the Main Track Papers
is: August 12, 2025 (Extended)
The below instructions are essential to ensure that you comply with the publication requirements set by IEEE in order for your paper(s) to be included in the 
						conference proceedings and published and indexed by IEEE.
Please make sure to incorporate all the reviewers' comments as that will be strictly monitored. In order to ensure that your paper is 
						included in the conference proceedings and will be submitted to appear in the IEEE Xplore, at least one of the authors must register and present the paper 
						either onsite or online.
The Final Camera Ready System
ICCNS is using the
PayQlick System
for both Camera ready submission and Registration.
Camera Ready Submission
1. Manuscript Format and Length
Your PDF final manuscript version and the electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF) must be submitted through
PayQlick System
. Please make sure your paper length is
8 pages for full papers, 6 pages for short papers and 2 pages for poster papers (all including references)
. Papers that exceed the length limit must pay the overlength charges.
Please make use of the appropriate IEEE template for conference proceedings to prepare your revised manuscript. Failur to do so may result in excluding your paper from the conference proceedings.
The needed IEEE Word template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Word Template)
.
The needed IEEE Latex template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Latex Template)
.
DO NOT include headers/footers or page numbers in the final submission.
2. Registartion
Please complete your paper(s) registration on-time. Registration instructions and rates are found at:
https://events.payqlick.com/event/41/ieee-iccns-2025
NO PAPER WILL BE PUBLISHED WITHOUT THE AUTHOR’S COMPLETED REGISTRATION AND PAYMENT.
3. Generate an IEEE XPLORE Compatible Manuscript Using PDF XPRESS
All final manuscripts must be generated using the PDF Xpress tool. Upload your camera-ready manuscript in one of many formats supported by the service and download your validated PDF when ready. To generate the IEEE Xplore compatible PDF file of your paper, follow these steps:
Log in to the IEEE PDF eXpress site:
https://ieee-pdf-express.org/
First-time users should do the following:
Select create account
Enter the following:
66249X
for the Conference ID
Your email address
A password
Continue to enter information as prompted. An Online confirmation will be displayed and an email confirmation will be sent verifying your account setup.
Once you have registered as a new user:
Upload the source file (containing your paper) for conversion.
Receive by e-mail the IEEE Xplore-compatible PDF of your paper for download.
If your paper coversion is declined (Not approved by IEEE), the system will provide with the errors log. You need to fix it, and re-do the same process.
Previous users of PDF eXpress need to follow the above steps, but should enter the same password that was used for previous conferences. Verify that your contact information is valid.
4. Electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF)
The authors should complete the Electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF) which is available through
PayQlick System
. Once you complete your registration:
Go to view My Tickets
Submit your IEEE eCF
5. Camera-Ready version Submission
Finally, you should upload your IEEE Xplore compatible PDF manuscript to
PayQlick System
.
Go to view My Tickets
Upload your Camera Ready (Must be IEEE PDF Certified)
If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to contact us at:
info@iccns-conference.org

Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Call for Papers
SUBMIT A PAPER
With the advancements in wireless communication systems like Fifth Generation (5G), beyond 5G (B5G) and the Sixth Generation (6G), 
 new and unprecedent services will be available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver of the future digital transformation of our cities and 
 communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquities deployment of the Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of 
 the network and at the cloud. The computing infrastructure will be handling the processing of the data generated by the users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require
 an efficient and sustainable applications running on the computing\Networking infrastructure and also a smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components. 
 Artificial Intelligence (AI)  and its applications will play a significant role on the design, deployment, automation and management of the future services.  This will include the applications
 that will be running on the edge and the cloud servers, the networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system and the intelligent automation and 
 management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present the state of the 
 art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking and Services that is supported by Artificial Intelligence.

Submission Guidelines:
SUBMIT A PAPER
Paper format
All papers should be in PDF format. Please make use of the appropriate IEEE template for conference proceedings to 
				prepare your revised manuscript.
Failur to do so may result in excluding your paper from the conference proceedings
.
The needed IEEE Word template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Word Template)
.
The needed IEEE Latex template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Latex Template)
.
DO NOT include headers/footers or page numbers in the final submission
.
Length
A full paper must be of maximum length of 8 pages (including all figures, 
				tables and references). The reference pages must only contain references. Up to 2 aditional pages can be added for extra cost. Overlength papers will be rejected without review.
Originality
Papers submitted to ICCNS must be the original work of the authors. 
					The may not be simultaneously under review elsewhere. Publications that have been peer-reviewed 
					and have appeared at other conferences or workshops may not be submitted to ICCNS. Authors should 
					be aware that IEEE has a strict policy with regard to plagiarism
https://www.ieee.org/publications/rights/plagiarism/plagiarism-faq.html
The authors' prior work must be cited appropriately.
Author list
Please ensure that you submit your papers with the full and final 
					list of authors in the correct order. The author list registered for each submission is not 
					allowed to be changed in any way after the paper submission deadline.
Proofreading:
Please proofread your submission carefully. It is essential that 
					the language use  in the paper is clear and correct so that it is easily understandable. 
					(Either US English or UK English spelling conventions are acceptable.)
IMPORTANT: No-Show Policy
At least one author of each accepted paper is required to register 
					to the conference and present the paper. Only registered and presented papers will be published in 
					the conference proceedings. A paper not presented or presented by a non-author without prior written 
					approval by the TPC Chair will be removed from the final conference proceedings and no registration 
					refund can be made to the authors of these papers.
Publication:
Accepted papers will be submitted for inclusion into IEEE Xplore subject to meeting IEEE Xplore’s scope and quality requirements.
Earlier versions of the proceedings can be found on:
IEEEXplore

Link: CALLS
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Call for Papers
SUBMIT A PAPER
With the advancements in wireless communication systems like Fifth Generation (5G), beyond 5G (B5G) and the Sixth Generation (6G), 
 new and unprecedent services will be available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver of the future digital transformation of our cities and 
 communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquities deployment of the Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of 
 the network and at the cloud. The computing infrastructure will be handling the processing of the data generated by the users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require
 an efficient and sustainable applications running on the computing\Networking infrastructure and also a smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components. 
 Artificial Intelligence (AI)  and its applications will play a significant role on the design, deployment, automation and management of the future services.  This will include the applications
 that will be running on the edge and the cloud servers, the networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system and the intelligent automation and 
 management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present the state of the 
 art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking and Services that is supported by Artificial Intelligence.
Researchers from both the industry and academia are encouraged to submit their original research contributions in all major areas, which include, but not limited to:

Call for Workshop Proposals
ICCNS will host a series of workshops and is also inviting new workshop proposals. 
				The purpose of the workshops is to emphasize current and emerging topics of particular interest to the community.
Proposal Format
Each workshop proposal must include (maximum 4 pages, in PDF format):
Title of the workshop.
Workshop organizers (name, affiliation and short biography).
Scope and topics of the workshop.
Rationale:
Why the workshop is related to ICCNS.
Why the topic is important.
Why the workshop may attract a significant number of attendees.
Workshop details:
A draft call for papers (including organizers, program committee, and steering committee if any). Organizers are expected to be fully committed and physically present at the workshop.
Workshop tentative schedule (number of expected papers, duration full/half day, format talks/posters, etc.). We encourage full-day events that demonstrate the interest of the community in the proposed topic and guarantee the commitment of the organizers. Half-day proposals are also welcome but expected to provide a rationale for the proposed duration.
Names of potential participants and invited speakers (if any).
Workshop history: if there are past workshops, the history of the workshop.
Proposals and any other enquiries should be sent to
info@iccns-conference.org

Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026

REGISTRATION FEES AND POLICIES

Keynotes. 
Main conference and workshops papers need a full registration. Otherwise, the paper 
will be exculded from the proceedings.

=== PAGE: https://iccns-conference.org/2026/venue.php ===
Link: VENUE
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Conference Venue
THE
CONFERENCE VENUE
The Conference will take place between the 1
st
and the 4
th
September 2025 in the notorious Hotel & Casino "Cherno more",
which is located on bul. "Slivnitsa" 33, PO9000 in Varna, Bulgaria.
Hotel & Casino "Cherno more" takes advantage of its
unique location - both in the heart of the city and only meters away from the
beach and the sea. The hotel is superbly located on the main pedestrian
promenade with many sightseeing and entertainment possibility nearby, including
landmarks and museums, galleries, shops, pubs, clubs and the famous Varna Sea
Garden.
One of the tallest and
most emblematic buildings of Varna, Hotel and Casino “Cherno More”, which
translates to "Black Sea" from Bulgarian language, has celebrated its
45 years in 2023.
The building was
designed by arch. Simeon Dimitrov and was opened on the 24
th
May
1978. The hotel was a large-scale and ambitious project for its time. Located
on the pedestrian promenade in the ideal Centre of Varna, the hotel has always
been closely connected with the cultural life of the city, in its role as a
main partner and place of accommodation for major events - festivals, music
events, conferences and seminars. Hotel & Casino "Cherno More" is
the preferred choice for casual travellers, business trips and events and for
all who wish to visit Varna, the Northern Black Sea and Bulgaria.
The 14-storey Cherno more hotel offers 199 rooms refurbished
elegantly, to a refined, high standard, for a comfortable and enjoyable stay.
All hotel rooms have balconies with wonderful sea and city views. Some of the
hotel rooms can accommodate guests on extra beds. Whether you visit Varna for a
business trip or a vacation near the sea, each of the rooms provides comfort
and coziness suitable for work or leisure. Guests have high-speed internet
access throughout the complex.
THE
CITY OF VARNA
Varna is the third-largest city in Bulgaria and the largest city and
seaside resort on the Bulgarian Black Sea Coast and in the Northern Bulgaria
region. Situated strategically in the Gulf of Varna, the city has been a major
economic, social and cultural centre for almost three millennia. Historically
known as Odessos, Varna developed from a Thracian seaside settlement into a
major seaport on the Black Sea.
Varna is an important centre for business, transportation,
education, tourism, entertainment, and healthcare. The city is referred to as
the maritime capital of Bulgaria and has the headquarters of the Bulgarian Navy
and merchant marine. In 2008, Varna was designated as the seat of the Black Sea
Euroregion by the Council of Europe. In 2014, Varna was awarded the title of
European Youth Capital.
The city landmarks include the Varna Archaeological Museum,
exhibiting the golden treasures of Varna, the Roman Baths, the Battle of Varna
Park Museum, the Naval Museum in the Italianate Villa Assareto, which is
displaying the museum ship and former torpedo boat “Drazki”, the Museum of
Ethnography in an Ottoman-period compound, which is featuring the life of local
urban dwellers, fisherfolk, and peasants in the late 19
th
and early
20
th
century. The 'Sea Garden' of Varna is the oldest and perhaps
largest park in the town that contains an open-air theatre, the Varna Aquarium,
which was opened in 1932, the Festa Dolphinarium, which was opened in 1984, the
Nicolaus Copernicus Observatory and Planetarium, the Museum of Natural History,
a terrarium, a zoo, an alpineum, a children's amusement park with a pond,
boathouse and ice-skating rink, as well as other attractions.
ENTERING
BULGARIA
Prior
to your journey, please pay attention that since 1 January 2007, the Republic
of Bulgaria is part of the European Union (EU) and is implementing the Common
Visa Policy of the EU.
According
to the EU Council Decision 2024/210 of 30 December 2023 on the full application
of the provisions of the Schengen acquis in the Republic of Bulgaria and
Romania, as of 31 March 2024, the Republic of Bulgaria is part of the Schengen
area by initially abolishing checks on persons at internal air and sea borders.
According to EU Council Decision 2024/210 of 12 December 2024 setting the date
for the lifting of checks on persons at internal land borders with and between
the Republic of Bulgaria and Romania, as of 1 January 2025, the checks on
persons at internal land borders have been lifted.
As
the Republic of Bulgaria is part of the EU and the Schengen Area, some guests
and the participants from specific countries might need to get visa for
entering the country. More information about the visa for Bulgaria can be found
at the webpage of the Bulgarian Ministry of Foreign Affairs:
https://www.mfa.bg/en/services-travel/consular-services/travel-bulgaria/visa-bulgaria
INVITATION
LETTERS
Invitation
letters will be issued to all participants following the acceptance of their
papers! For the issuing of the Invitation Letter you must provide the title of
the paper and a valid copy/photo of your International passport. Please send
all inquiries and questions to email:
pzahariev@uni-ruse.bg
TRAVEL
INFORMATION
The Conference will
take place between the 1
st
and the 4
th
September 2025 in
the notorious Hotel & Casino "Cherno more", which is located on
bul. "Slivnitsa" 33, PO9000 in Varna, Bulgaria -
https://maps.app.goo.gl/33BoFjkjfHySKdr97
, GPS Coordinates: 43°20’50.0″N  and 27°91’99.7″E - (DD)
43.20500, 27.91997. You can reach the venue, from:
VARNA INTERNATIONAL
AIRPORT, VARNA, BULGARIA
Varna International
Airport is approximately 11 km away from Hotel & Casino “Cherno More”. Bus
line №409 will take you directly from the bus terminal at the airport to the
closest possible bus station near the hotel – Station “Sevastopol”, which is
just 350 meters away from the venue. More info about the buses you can find
here:
https://www.varnatraffic.com/en/Line/Routes/409
SOFIA INTERNATIONAL
AIRPORT, SOFIA, BULGARIA
Sofia International
Airport is located in the Capitol of Bulgaria and is approximately 500 km away
from the city of Varna. You can reach Varna from the Sofia International
Airport either by renting a car (approximate travel time between 5 hours and 30
minutes and 6 hours) or by using public transport. More information here:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/tbZdqTvjc81PQ8nb9
BURGAS AIRPORT, BURGAS,
BULGARIA
Burgas Airport is located
in the second largest city on the Bulgarian seacoast and is approximately 120
km away from the city of Varna. You can reach Varna from the Burgas Airport
either by renting a car (approximate travel time between 2 hours and 2 hours
and 30 minutes) or by using public transport. More information here:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/dZEGMcPQTXnJUSWG8
HENRI COANDA
INTERNATIONAL AIRPORT, BUCHAREST, ROMANIA
Bucharest International
Airport is located approx. 300 km away from Varna. The airport, named Henri
Coanda International Airport, is located in Otopeni, 16.5 km north of the Bucharest
city centre. The airport supports flights from more than 30 companies and is
the best solution to reach Varna by airplane. You can reach Varna from the Bucharest
International Airport either by renting a car (approximate travel time between
4 hours and 5 hours) or by using public transport. More information here:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/XYSkVvKp9Pti9SxU9
ISTANBUL HAVALIMANI
AIRPORT, ISTANBUL, TURKEY
Istanbul Havalimani
Airport is the larger of two international airports serving Istanbul, Turkey.
It is located in the Arnavutköy district on the European side of the city. It
is the largest airport in Turkey and the 2
nd
busiest airport in
Europe as well as the 2
nd
busiest airport in the Middle East.
You can reach Varna
from the Istanbul Havalimani Airport either by renting a car (approximate
travel time between 6 hours and 8 hours) or by using public transport. More
information here:
https://maps.app.goo.gl/GCmdpdHRmM8NAqBXA
If you are
having travel-related questions or if you need us to assist you with your
travel – please contact a member of the local organization committee:
e-mail:
pzahariev@uni-ruse.bg
, mobile: +359 82 888 663 and tel.: +359 896 698 699
ACCOMMODATION
HOTEL & CASINO “CHERNO
MORE”
The conference
organizers have blocked a limited number of Double Classic, Twin Classic and
Twin Deluxe rooms, which are to be used by the participants in the conference.
To book one of these rooms, please contact us on e-mail:
pzahariev@uni-ruse.bg
Additional
rooms in Hotel & Casino “Cherno More” can be booked at the official webpage
of the Hotel -
https://www.chernomorebg.com/en/
or here:
https://www.booking.com/hotel/bg/hotel-casino-cherno-more.en-gb.html
ROSSLYN
DIMYAT HOTEL VARNA
The Rosslyn Dimyat
Hotel Varna is located in the central part of our seaside capital, conveniently
located between some of the most important key sites and attractions in the
city, making it an even more attractive place to stay during your business trip
or seaside holiday.
The hotel is just a
step away from Varna's Sea Garden, famous for its green areas, beautiful flower
alleys and tennis courts. Most of the hotel rooms offer a magnificent sea view
which you can enjoy up close during your walk along the coast just minutes walking
distance from the hotel.
Rooms can be
booked at the official webpage of the Hotel -
https://dimyat.rosslyn-hotels.com/en/
or here:
https://www.booking.com/hotel/bg/dimyat-hotel-varna.en-gb.html
OTHER
OPTIONS
Other
booking options can be explored here:
https://www.booking.com/searchresults.en-gb.html?ss=Varna+City&ssne=Varna+City&ssne_untouched=Varna+City&efdco=1
If you are
having any accommodation-related questions or if you need us to assist you with

pzahariev@uni-ruse.bg
, mobile: +359 82 888 663 and tel.:
+359 896 698 699

Link: AUTHORS
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Submission Guidelines:
SUBMIT A PAPER
Paper format
All papers should be in PDF format. Please make use of the appropriate IEEE template for conference proceedings to 
				prepare your revised manuscript.
Failur to do so may result in excluding your paper from the conference proceedings
.
The needed IEEE Word template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Word Template)
.
The needed IEEE Latex template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Latex Template)
.
DO NOT include headers/footers or page numbers in the final submission
.
Length
A full paper must be of maximum length of 8 pages (including all figures, 
				tables and references). The reference pages must only contain references. Up to 2 aditional pages can be added for extra cost. Overlength papers will be rejected without review.
Originality
Papers submitted to ICCNS must be the original work of the authors. 
					The may not be simultaneously under review elsewhere. Publications that have been peer-reviewed 
					and have appeared at other conferences or workshops may not be submitted to ICCNS. Authors should 
					be aware that IEEE has a strict policy with regard to plagiarism
https://www.ieee.org/publications/rights/plagiarism/plagiarism-faq.html
The authors' prior work must be cited appropriately.
Author list
Please ensure that you submit your papers with the full and final 
					list of authors in the correct order. The author list registered for each submission is not 
					allowed to be changed in any way after the paper submission deadline.
Proofreading:
Please proofread your submission carefully. It is essential that 
					the language use  in the paper is clear and correct so that it is easily understandable. 
					(Either US English or UK English spelling conventions are acceptable.)
IMPORTANT: No-Show Policy
At least one author of each accepted paper is required to register 
					to the conference and present the paper. Only registered and presented papers will be published in 
					the conference proceedings. A paper not presented or presented by a non-author without prior written 
					approval by the TPC Chair will be removed from the final conference proceedings and no registration 
					refund can be made to the authors of these papers.
Publication:
Accepted papers will be submitted for inclusion into IEEE Xplore subject to meeting IEEE Xplore’s scope and quality requirements.
Earlier versions of the proceedings can be found on:
IEEEXplore

Welcome to The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Comunications, Networking and Services (ICCNS2023)
Call for Papers
New advancements in wireless communication systems such as Fifth-Generation (5G), Beyond Fifth-Generation (B5G), and Sixth-Generation (6G) networks will allow for new and unprecedented services to be made available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver for future digital transformation of our cities and communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquitous deployment of Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of the network and in the cloud. This computing infrastructure will handle the processing of data generated by users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require the applications running on the computing/networking infrastructure to be Intelligent, efficient and sustainable. Additionally, the infrastructure will require smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components. Artificial Intelligence (AI) and its applications will play a significant role in the design, deployment, automation, and management of future services. This will include applications that will be running on the edge and on cloud servers, networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system, and intelligent automation and management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking, and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present state of the art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking, and Services that are supported by Artificial Intelligence.

Link: CAMERA READY
Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Camera ready Guidelines
The Deadline for submitting the
Camera-ready
version for the Main Track Papers
is: August 12, 2025 (Extended)
The below instructions are essential to ensure that you comply with the publication requirements set by IEEE in order for your paper(s) to be included in the 
						conference proceedings and published and indexed by IEEE.
Please make sure to incorporate all the reviewers' comments as that will be strictly monitored. In order to ensure that your paper is 
						included in the conference proceedings and will be submitted to appear in the IEEE Xplore, at least one of the authors must register and present the paper 
						either onsite or online.
The Final Camera Ready System
ICCNS is using the
PayQlick System
for both Camera ready submission and Registration.
Camera Ready Submission
1. Manuscript Format and Length
Your PDF final manuscript version and the electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF) must be submitted through
PayQlick System
. Please make sure your paper length is
8 pages for full papers, 6 pages for short papers and 2 pages for poster papers (all including references)
. Papers that exceed the length limit must pay the overlength charges.
Please make use of the appropriate IEEE template for conference proceedings to prepare your revised manuscript. Failur to do so may result in excluding your paper from the conference proceedings.
The needed IEEE Word template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Word Template)
.
The needed IEEE Latex template can be found her
(IEEE Conference Latex Template)
.
DO NOT include headers/footers or page numbers in the final submission.
2. Registartion
Please complete your paper(s) registration on-time. Registration instructions and rates are found at:
https://events.payqlick.com/event/41/ieee-iccns-2025
NO PAPER WILL BE PUBLISHED WITHOUT THE AUTHOR’S COMPLETED REGISTRATION AND PAYMENT.
3. Generate an IEEE XPLORE Compatible Manuscript Using PDF XPRESS
All final manuscripts must be generated using the PDF Xpress tool. Upload your camera-ready manuscript in one of many formats supported by the service and download your validated PDF when ready. To generate the IEEE Xplore compatible PDF file of your paper, follow these steps:
Log in to the IEEE PDF eXpress site:
https://ieee-pdf-express.org/
First-time users should do the following:
Select create account
Enter the following:
66249X
for the Conference ID
Your email address
A password
Continue to enter information as prompted. An Online confirmation will be displayed and an email confirmation will be sent verifying your account setup.
Once you have registered as a new user:
Upload the source file (containing your paper) for conversion.
Receive by e-mail the IEEE Xplore-compatible PDF of your paper for download.
If your paper coversion is declined (Not approved by IEEE), the system will provide with the errors log. You need to fix it, and re-do the same process.
Previous users of PDF eXpress need to follow the above steps, but should enter the same password that was used for previous conferences. Verify that your contact information is valid.
4. Electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF)
The authors should complete the Electronic IEEE Copyright Form (eCF) which is available through
PayQlick System
. Once you complete your registration:
Go to view My Tickets
Submit your IEEE eCF
5. Camera-Ready version Submission
Finally, you should upload your IEEE Xplore compatible PDF manuscript to
PayQlick System
.
Go to view My Tickets
Upload your Camera Ready (Must be IEEE PDF Certified)
If you have any questions or concerns, please feel free to contact us at:
info@iccns-conference.org

Title: ICCNS2026
ICCNS2026
The 4th International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Communication, Networking and Services (ICCNS2026)
22-25 Sept. 2026 | Bucharest, Romania.
Call for Papers
SUBMIT A PAPER
With the advancements in wireless communication systems like Fifth Generation (5G), beyond 5G (B5G) and the Sixth Generation (6G), 
 new and unprecedent services will be available for users with nearly unlimited capacity. These services will be the core driver of the future digital transformation of our cities and 
 communities. This will be accompanied by a ubiquities deployment of the Internet of Things (IoT) infrastructure and supported by computing capacity that will be available at the edge of 
 the network and at the cloud. The computing infrastructure will be handling the processing of the data generated by the users and services. Such a complex and diverse system will require
 an efficient and sustainable applications running on the computing\Networking infrastructure and also a smart control and automation systems to integrate and manage its different components. 
 Artificial Intelligence (AI)  and its applications will play a significant role on the design, deployment, automation and management of the future services.  This will include the applications
 that will be running on the edge and the cloud servers, the networking applications to handle the flow of data between the users and the computing system and the intelligent automation and 
 management software operating on the system. The International Conference on Intelligent Computing, Networking and Services is aiming to provide an opportunity to present the state of the 
 art research in the intersections of Computing, Networking and Services that is supported by Artificial Intelligence.
Researchers from both the industry and academia are encouraged to submit their original research contributions in all major areas, which include, but not limited to:

Paper format

.
